assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been declared and recognised as an intragroup receivable/payable, so it is a recognised monetary item.

The receivable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation because the relevant group entities have different functional currencies.
The designation being assessed is limited to the foreign currency risk component of that recognised item.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised asset, so its foreign currency risk component is relevant to assess under the fair value hedge model in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also contemplates cash flow hedges for foreign currency risk on recognised assets or liabilities, making this a distinct hedge accounting model to consider.
